hard_filter_check.notes: No hard filter failures. Mining & aggregate is explicitly in ICP. US operations confirmed. Capital project scale confirmed.

criteria_scores.reasoning: FCX is the largest US copper producer. Mining & aggregate is explicitly listed in ISCO's ICP. ISCO recently supplied the Collahuasi copper mine (Chile) — direct comparable.

criteria_scores.criterion: Capital project scale — large or complex piping requirement
criteria_scores.reasoning: Bagdad (AZ) expansion = $3.5B capital investment. El Abra (Chile) mill = 700M lbs/yr capacity. Total 2026 capex = $4.3B. Scale is top-decile. Copper mines require miles of HDPE slurry pipeline, process water, and tailings management systems.

criteria_scores.criterion: Technical decision-maker on staff (engineer or PM role)
criteria_scores.reasoning: FCX operates 9 US mines. Each major mine has in-house engineering, project management, and procurement teams. Bagdad expansion will require dedicated capital projects team.

criteria_scores.criterion: Active capital spend or project pipeline
criteria_scores.reasoning: $4.3B total capex 2026 confirmed in Q1 2026 earnings release (primary source: SEC-filed PDF). Bagdad expansion decision H2 2026 — procurement window opening now.

criteria_scores.criterion: US-based or international project with US procurement
criteria_scores.reasoning: HQ: Phoenix, AZ. Primary operations: AZ (Morenci, Bagdad, Sierrita, Miami, Safford/Lone Star), NM (Chino, Tyrone), NV (Henderson), CO (Climax). Procurement for US mines done from US.

fit_reasoning: FCX is a textbook ICP for ISCO. Mining & aggregate vertical, $4.3B capex confirmed, 9 US mine sites each requiring HDPE infrastructure, technical procurement teams in place, and a $3.5B expansion decision imminent at Bagdad (AZ). No hard filters failed.
priority_note: Tier 1 / Score 10. Run signal research immediately. Bagdad expansion decision is H2 2026 — procurement window for pipe and fusion equipment likely opening Q3 2026.
